scope_boundary: Bounded public deployed-site attestation for one Artifact Graphs GitHub Pages URL. The tracked result records a successful HTTP fetch, content digest, response metadata, and required record-reality and non-claim fragments from the public site. It is not independent external human review, not deployed attestation behavior, not open-world receipt faithfulness, not verifier-correctness evidence, not reader release approval, and not an upward support-state transition.
transition_reason: The public deployed-site attestation result artifact-public-site-record-reality-attestation-2026-07-05 is useful because it moves the Artifact Graphs record-reality lane beyond local files and CI-service status: the public GitHub Pages URL served the Artifact Graphs chapter with the expected record-reality ladder, epistemic-TCB boundary, CI attestation section, live/randomized attestation probe references, and explicit non-claim boundaries. The evidence remains served-page evidence for one URL at one fetch time, so it blocks promotion for deployed attestation, open-world receipt faithfulness, verifier correctness, reader release approval, external human review, or chapter-core support.
